description: Committing a .env file with live values puts production credentials into version control where everyone with the repo can read them.
body: Failure mode: a .env holding real credentials is added to the repo, often because it was not gitignored, leaking secrets to all who clone. Detection signals: a .env with non-placeholder values is tracked; a scan flags credentials inside it; collaborators have the file locally. Do not keep a credential-bearing .env in version control. Safer fallback: rotate every secret it contained, remove and gitignore the file, provide a placeholder example file instead, load real values from the environment or a manager, and scrub history after rotating.
